rag_rules: When fixing type mismatches in AGREE contracts, ensure left and right sides of binary expressions have matching types.
Only use assign statements for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
Align helper declaration types with the types of their assigned expressions, especially for comparisons and arithmetic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding architecture elements or new types when resolving AGREE diagnostics; focus on fixing existing declarations.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assign for output bindings.
